title: Decoy Squad Five
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Squad Five}} {{Update|{{EA|url=games/starwars/galaxy-of-heroes/news/kit-reveal-disguised-clone-trooper|text=Kit Reveal: Disguised Clone Trooper}}}} {{Organization |image=[[File:DisguisedCloneTrooper-SWGoH.png]] |name=Decoy Squad Five |type=[[Disguised Clone Trooper/Legends|Disguised Clone Trooper]] squad{{EA|url=games/starwars/galaxy-of-heroes/news/kit-reveal-disguised-clone-trooper|text=Kit Reveal: Disguised Clone Trooper}} |founder= |leader=A [[Unidentified clone trooper sergeant (Decoy Squad Five)|clone trooper sergeant]][[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]] |subgroup= |headquarters= |locations= |formed= |founded= |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of the [[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]],—including Decoy Squad Five's placement at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]— in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. |restored= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] }} {{Quote|Anyone could grab some deformed kid and claim it's anything they want. How do you know it's a Jedi?''"
"''My lightsaber, the first clue would be, hmm?|Yoda reveals himself to the leader of Decoy Squad Five|}} '''Decoy Squad Five''' was an eight-member [[Squad/Legends|detachment]] of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] from the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], led by a [[Unidentified clone trooper sergeant (Decoy Squad Five)|sergeant]]. The squad's mission in the wake of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] and [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]], a planned assault against the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] all across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at the conclusion of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], was to eliminate any Jedi who attempted to return to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. To that end, it was one of several squads that had been outfitted with [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robe]] disguises and acted as [[Disguised Clone Trooper/Legends|Disguised Clone Troopers]]. The squad's [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] had been bred to possess very quick reflexes and to be constantly alert. The members of Decoy Squad Five were [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] when the two infiltrated the Temple to terminate a [[Jedi beacon/Legends|false homing signal]] that had been activated by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. ==History== {{Quote|Oooh, sher. Sher, Yer Grace. Jedi babby, sher azzell iddiz! Come from outcher Temple, dinnit? Lookit!|A disguised Obi-Wan Kenobi to the leader of Decoy Squad Five|}} A detachment of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], Decoy Squad Five was one of several[[Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith 4|''Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith'' 4]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] [[Squad/Legends|squads]] disguised in [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]], acting as [[Disguised Clone Trooper/Legends|Disguised Clone Troopers]]. The squad was deployed at the downlevel loading dock of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant's]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. The clones, led by a [[Unidentified clone trooper sergeant (Decoy Squad Five)|sergeant]], guarded the entrance to the Temple after the enacting of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], a measure trained into the clones that ordered them to kill their Jedi [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] in the event that they were considered to have become a threat to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' After the subsequent [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]]—[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] march on the Temple that resulted in the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of almost all of the Jedi inside—a [[Jedi beacon/Legends|beacon]] was sent to all surviving Jedi informing them that the war was over and that they were to return to the Temple. Decoy Squad Five was posted at the dock to intercept and kill the returning Jedi. The eight clones were approached by a [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|hunchbacked man]], who drooled constantly and spoke indistinctly, carrying a bundle in his arms. The troopers drew their weapons and commanded him to identify himself, but he assured them that he was on their side, insisting that he had captured a [[Yoda/Legends|Jedi baby]] for them to execute. Curious, the [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] took a closer look and judged it the ugliest-looking baby he had ever seen, questioning if it was really a Jedi. The "baby", actually [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] Yoda in disguise, chose that moment to speak, suggesting that his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] would prove his identity before holding his [[Color/Legends|green]] blade to the Sergeant's face. The hunchbacked man, Obi-Wan Kenobi, straightened and started speaking with a standard and dignified [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]] accent, telling the group that if none resisted then they would not be harmed. The men of Decoy Squad Five would not comply and were cut down by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] within six seconds. Yoda and Kenobi decided not to hide the group's bodies because they had no time to spare while [[Infiltration of the Jedi Temple/Legends|infiltrating]] the Temple, so they continued inside. They figured that if the clones were perceived as leaving their post, it would be just as odd as finding their bodies in the seedy lower levels of Coruscant. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Clone_in_Jedi_disguise.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Production photo of a disguised clone]] A production photo included on the [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']] DVD shows [[Temuera Morrison]] brandishing a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] while disguised in Jedi robes.{{Film|III}}, DVD extras. In the [[2011]] ''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' Blu-ray collection, the first extra features disc contains an early animation test with the disguised clones. All but one of the clones were animated. The Kenobi that appeared with them was not animated.''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'', Disc Seven. Although they were not included in the film, a group of these clones called Decoy Squad Five appeared in the 2005 [[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|novelization]], which means they are [[canon]] with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, and, in [[2025]], would appear playable in ''[[Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes]]'', with the official kit reveal identifying the soldier as a member of Decoy Squad Five. Clones disguised as Jedi were also mentioned in the [[Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith (comic series)|fourth issue]] of the 2005 [[Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith (comic series)|''Episode III'' comic]]. These clones were also cut from the 2005 [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|video game adaptation]]. The game includes concept art of the clones meeting Obi-Wan and Yoda in the Temple courtyard before throwing off their robes and opening fire on the Jedi.[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' video game]] Clones disguised as Jedi first appeared in ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Video Game]]'', released one month before ''Episode III''. "Disguised Clone" is an unlockable character. He wears a brown Jedi-style cloak and hood piece instead of his helmet, but wears the typical clone-armor body piece underneath. He functions like a normal clone trooper.''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Video Game]]'' This clone also appears in the ''LEGO Star Wars'' compilation game, ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' (2007).''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' The idea of Clones hunting an infant member of Yoda's species during Order 66 was introduced into the Canon timeline in ''The Book of Boba Fett'', though in this case it was in fact a legitimate baby Yoda, not the nearly 900 year old Grandmaster.{{BOBF|Chapter 6: From the Desert Comes a Stranger}} ==Appearances== *{{Film|III}} {{Del}} *[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]] {{Ab|Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' video game]] {{C|Cut}} {{C|Appears as concept art}} *[[Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith 4|''Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith'' 4]] {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes]]'' ===Non-canon appearances=== *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Video Game]]'' *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{EA|url=games/starwars/galaxy-of-heroes/news/kit-reveal-disguised-clone-trooper|text=Kit Reveal: Disguised Clone Trooper}} *{{EA|subdomain=forums|url=blog/swgoh-game-info-hub-en/kit-reveal-disguised-clone-trooper/12138321|text=Kit Reveal: Disguised Clone Trooper}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Ködergruppe Fünf |es=Escuadrón Señuelo Cinco |tr=Yem Mangası Beş }} [[Category:501st Legion units (Galactic Republic)]] [[Category:Grand Army of the Republic squads]]
